author: m.kalinin
content:
What if we include proposal step into FC tests? With this the `payloadAttributes` a client sends to EL can be checked. `get_proposer_head` can be used to get the head that the proposal would use in each case. This would make FC aware of Engine API, but it's probably fine. Then we could add this step to our compliance test generator and create manual tests checking particular edge cases.

It's not necessarily should be a real proposal, but rather a check on the payload attributes and proposer head.

author: tersec
content: as long as the tests can still be run with only a CL, in isolation

author: tersec
content: i.e. once it becomes a "real" network test might as well use mechanisms designed to orchestrate that better (Hive, Kurtosis, etc)

author: m.kalinin
content: I see the problem Potuz described as the following: proposal routine isn't coherent with the fork choice and calls EL with inconsistent data. Doing a basic check on the data doesn't require a real EL here

author: tersec
content: yes, agree

author: tersec
content: one general note is that right now, Nimbus (nor any other CL) actually can't use FCR with Nethermind: https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/issues/11185

author: m.kalinin
content:
> I feel that creating scenarios and actually implementing them in client images to run in Kurtosis quickly blows up in complexity. 

This is one of the major problems with the FC tests as we have a very large variated of system states. Devnets help to address, but i don't see any better way than fuzzing or model based (any other alternative) + fuzzing approach to tackle this problem. At least now

author: tersec
content: i.e. to some extent we'd fake things to pass any such EL-data test

author: tersec
content: because there's no other choice

author: m.kalinin
content: This should have a Hive test cc <@892053833121923094>

author: tersec
content: and/or just skip those parts of the tests (more likely I guess?)

author: m.kalinin
content: but the NM is an EL problem, not a CL one. And if there were a Hive test for this specific we would caught it much earlier

author: tersec
content: doesn't matter we can't ship it

author: tersec
content: and for test purposes, see above

author: m.kalinin
content: I am not sure I get your point. Are you saying that we need a test env with real EL regardless?

author: tersec
content: no

author: tersec
content: I'm saying that the way Nimbus would have to test this is to one of (a) not provide the test the data it would provide to the EL; (b) intentionally provide ELs data which gets Nethermind wedged; or (c) skip that part of the test

author: tersec
content: i.e. it wouldn't test what the CL does its usual flow

author: m.kalinin
content: I see

author: nc1234
content: Did you pass fork choice's `on_execution_payload_envelope__wrong_withdrawals` ? I pass everything except this. This looks identical to  `on_execution_payload_envelope__valid`, same anchor state, apply the same block, the same execution payload envelope, yet one expect it to be invalid, and the other one expect it to be valid

author: nc1234
content: created a PR to fix this https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5133
